title: Rollback strategy documentation
scope: Rollback and containment procedures for each irreversible migration phase identified during W2-01 through W2-03 execution.

phases.name: SOC Dashboard: SQLite-to-PostgreSQL full schema deployment
irreversible_operations: Column type promotions: INTEGER→BOOLEAN (flag columns), TEXT→JSONB (details/metadata), DATETIME→TIMESTAMPTZ
NOT NULL and CHECK constraints added; no implicit cast back to SQLite equivalents
RLS policies and row-level ownership enforce access model that cannot be trivially reverted without schema migration
If SQLite file is deleted/overwritten: source data gone
pre_migration_checklist: Confirm Docker/PostgreSQL container healthy: docker ps | grep postgres
Run migration replay validation (W2-01): 21/21 checks must pass
Confirm app version pinned to known-good commit; store commit SHA
Record pre-migration row counts: users, agents, incidents, triage_rules, m365_tenants, audit_log
Take pg_dump baseline immediately after schema apply and before any app writes
phases.rollback_window: Zero-downtime rollback possible only BEFORE first production write to PostgreSQL (i.e. within the deploy changeover window, typically <15 min). After first production write, data diverges.

rollback_procedure.trigger: Health check failure, query errors, or P1 incident within first 15 minutes of cutover
steps: 1. Stop app process: systemctl stop soc-dashboard (or equivalent)
2. Revert app to prior known-good commit: git checkout <pre-migration-sha>
3. Point DATABASE_URL env var back to SQLite connection string (or revert .env)
4. Restart app: systemctl start soc-dashboard
5. Validate: curl /health returns 200; key routes respond
6. Preserve PostgreSQL container (do NOT destroy) for post-incident forensics
7. Raise incident per W6-03 runbook; notify program lead
rollback_procedure.data_loss_risk: None — SQLite backup preserved and PostgreSQL writes not yet committed to production data flow

containment_procedure.trigger: Rollback window expired; data already written to PostgreSQL in production
steps: 1. DO NOT attempt live rollback — data divergence would cause inconsistency
2. Activate read-only mode: block write endpoints via feature flag or nginx rewrite
3. Take immediate pg_dump: pg_dump $DATABASE_URL > /opt/soc/emergency-$(date +%Y%m%dT%H%M%S).sql
4. Identify diverging rows via audit_log timestamps (all writes post-cutover)
5. For minor schema issues: apply forward-fix migration and redeploy
6. For major data corruption: restore from pre-migration pg_dump baseline; replay audit_log events if possible
7. Coordinate with program lead before any destructive action
containment_procedure.data_loss_risk: Medium — writes made between cutover and containment action may require manual reconciliation against SQLite backup

go_no_go_criteria: SQLite backup verified readable and row counts match expectations
pg_dump baseline taken and stored off-host
On-call engineer available for 1-hour cutover window
Rollback runbook rehearsed (dry-run) in staging first

phases.name: SOC Dashboard: Admin seed delegation fix (commit 403f5cf)
irreversible_operations: Removed INSERT for admin user with placeholder 'REPLACE_WITH_HASHED_PASSWORD' from schema.sql
Fresh deploys now rely entirely on init_default_admin() at app startup to seed admin
Existing deployments: admin row already in DB — no data loss, but password hash is REPLACE_WITH_HASHED_PASSWORD if inserted before fix
pre_migration_checklist: Verify admin user row in production DB: SELECT id, username, password_hash FROM users WHERE role='admin'
If password_hash = 'REPLACE_WITH_HASHED_PASSWORD': admin login is broken — fix is urgent
If admin row absent: init_default_admin() will create correct row on next restart
Confirm ADMIN_PASSWORD env var is set in production .env before restart
phases.rollback_window: N/A — this fix is safe; the only rollback scenario is reverting to broken placeholder behavior (not recommended)

rollback_procedure.trigger: init_default_admin() fails to create admin row on fresh deploy
steps: 1. Check app startup logs for init_default_admin() errors
2. If function errors: run manually via python3 -c "from supabase.users import init_default_admin; init_default_admin()"
3. If env var missing: set ADMIN_PASSWORD in .env and restart
4. Verify: SELECT id FROM users WHERE role='admin' returns a row
1. Connect directly to PostgreSQL: psql $DATABASE_URL
2. Check current admin row: SELECT username, password_hash FROM users WHERE role='admin';
3. If hash is placeholder: UPDATE users SET password_hash = '<bcrypt-hash-of-admin-pw>' WHERE role='admin';
4. Alternatively: DELETE FROM users WHERE role='admin'; and restart app to let init_default_admin() recreate
5. Confirm login works before closing incident

go_no_go_criteria: ADMIN_PASSWORD env var set in production environment
init_default_admin() tested on fresh schema in W2-01 replay (confirmed passing)
No admin row with placeholder hash exists in production DB before this deploy

phases.name: SimplyClik Platform API: request_status_events migration + backfill
irreversible_operations: CREATE TABLE request_status_events with FK to requests — table exists post-migration
INSERT backfill: one 'submitted' event per existing request row (WHERE NOT EXISTS guard makes it idempotent, but once run in prod, data exists)
Code changes to POST /requests and PATCH /requests/:requestId/status emit events atomically — after cutover, every state change writes an event; rollback would leave orphaned events
Timeline endpoint (GET /requests/:id/timeline) now returns events — clients depending on this shape cannot easily revert
pre_migration_checklist: Verify requests table row count: SELECT count(*) FROM requests;
Dry-run backfill with COUNT: SELECT count(*) FROM requests WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM request_status_events WHERE request_id=requests.id);
Confirm index on request_status_events(request_id) is present in migration
Take Supabase point-in-time backup via dashboard before applying migration
Confirm migration is idempotent: second run of backfill INSERT must produce 0 rows inserted
Review API version strategy: if clients are on old contract during migration window, events emit but clients ignore — safe
phases.rollback_window: Rollback possible BEFORE first organic event write (i.e. before any POST /requests or PATCH status call hits the new code path in production). After organic events exist, dropping the table loses legitimate data.

rollback_procedure.trigger: Migration failure, FK violation, or query performance degradation immediately after apply
steps: 1. Apply down-migration: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS request_status_events CASCADE;
(safe only if no organic events written yet — check: SELECT count(*) FROM request_status_events;)
2. Revert platform-api code to commit prior to PR #7 merge
3. Redeploy platform-api (zero-downtime via rolling restart in CI/CD)
4. Verify: GET /v1/requests/:id/timeline returns 404 or empty (old behavior)
5. Notify via incident runbook (W6-03)
rollback_procedure.data_loss_risk: Low if caught before organic events — only backfilled data (reconstructible) is lost. High if organic events exist.

containment_procedure.trigger: Organic events already written; table cannot be dropped without data loss
steps: 1. Do NOT drop request_status_events — preserve all data
2. If issue is performance: add missing index; do not roll back table
3. If issue is data integrity (wrong backfill, future-timestamp events): run targeted DELETE on known-bad rows only
DELETE FROM request_status_events WHERE changed_at > now(); -- future timestamps
DELETE FROM request_status_events WHERE created_by_profile_id IS NULL AND changed_at < '2026-06-05'; -- orphaned backfill rows if needed
4. If issue is FK constraint failure: identify orphaned request_id values and reconcile
5. For timeline query bugs: apply forward-fix in a new migration; do not modify existing backfill data
6. Take pg_dump snapshot before any DELETE operations
containment_procedure.data_loss_risk: Low to Medium — targeted remediation avoids full rollback; legitimate event history preserved

go_no_go_criteria: W2-03 static verification: 6/7 backfill SQL checks pass (REQUIRED)
Migration idempotency confirmed: re-run produces 0 new inserts
Backfill dry-run count matches requests table count
Supabase point-in-time backup taken within 1 hour of migration apply
Platform-api CI passing on migration branch (build + test + integration-gate)
Rollback down-migration script tested in staging: table drops cleanly with CASCADE

cross_cutting_controls.backup_policy: pg_dump or Supabase PiTR snapshot taken within 1 hour before every schema-altering migration
cross_cutting_controls.migration_sequencing: All migrations applied in order: P1 (SOC schema) → P2 (admin fix restart) → P3 (platform API events). No concurrent schema changes.
cross_cutting_controls.change_window: Non-peak hours (22:00–05:00 local time). Cutover engineer on-call for minimum 1 hour post-apply.
cross_cutting_controls.communication: Slack/Teams alert to program lead and on-call 30 min before cutover; rollback decision authority delegated to on-call after 15 min window
cross_cutting_controls.evidence_requirement: Each phase must produce: pre-migration row counts, migration apply log, post-migration validation query results. Store in artifacts/.
cross_cutting_controls.staging_requirement: All phases must be rehearsed in staging environment before production apply.

summary.highest_rollback_risk: P3 - request_status_events backfill (organic event writes close rollback window within minutes of API cutover)
summary.recommended_sequence: Stage P1+P2 together (same service restart); P3 independently with separate change window
